section.heading: PFAS research and development

subsection.heading: In general
subsection.chapeau: The Administrator, acting through the Assistant Administrator for the Office of Research and Development, shall—

subparagraph.content: further examine the effects of perfluoroalkyl and polyfluoroalkyl substances on human health and the environment; and
make publicly available information relating to the findings under subparagraph (A);

paragraph.chapeau: develop a process for prioritizing which perfluoroalkyl and polyfluoroalkyl substances, or classes of perfluoroalkyl and polyfluoroalkyl substances, should be subject to additional research efforts that is based on—

subparagraph.content: the potential for human exposure to the substances or classes of substances;
the potential toxicity of the substances or classes of substances; and
information available about the substances or classes of substances;

paragraph.content: develop new tools to characterize and identify perfluoroalkyl and polyfluoroalkyl substances in the environment, including in drinking water, wastewater, surface water, groundwater, solids, and the air;
evaluate approaches for the remediation of contamination by perfluoroalkyl and polyfluoroalkyl substances in the environment; and
develop and implement new tools and materials to communicate with the public about perfluoroalkyl and polyfluoroalkyl substances.

content.p: There is authorized to be appropriated to the Administrator to carry out this section $15,000,000 for each of fiscal years 2020 through 2024.

section.sourceCredit: (Pub. L. 116–92, div. F, title LXXIII, § 7362, Dec. 20, 2019, 133 Stat. 2290.)
